MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Patrick M. Malone, Esq., of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4053), appearing for defendant / moving party Metalclad Insulation Corporation
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party James Mihalko
Christopher D. Strunk, Esq., of GORDON & REES (415-986-5900), appearing for defendant / joinder SBC Holdings, Inc.
James D. Fraser, Esq., of LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, (415-438-6658) for defendant Goodyear Tire and Rubber Company

RULING:
HEARING ON DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT and DEFENDANT SBC HOLDINGS, INC.'S JOINDER IN DEFENDANT METALCALD' S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Motions were argued. The Court further conducted a case management conference. Counsel reached an agreement: (1) the new motion for summary judgment will serve in 30 days notice without the 2 extra days. (2) Metalclad and SBC defendants will withdraw the current motions for summary judgment on Washington statute of limitations and file new motions on Washington and California statutes of limitations. All other defendants will have to follow the same terms if they want to file a new motion.
Defendants' new motions are to be filed on or before April 18, 2011. Hearing will be heard on May 18, 2011 at 9:30 a.m., opposition due on 5/4 and reply 5/11.
The Court ordered continued the trial date from March 14, 2011 to May 31, 2011 at 11:15 a.m. in Dept. 220. Discovery for percipient witnesses as to all issues other than the statutes of limitations are remain closed. Discovery re: the statute of limitations issue will be closed on 4/1 by the end of the day. Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

RULING:
DEFENDANT THE GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied as untimely. The prior order allowed summary judgment motions to be heard on 30 days notice. As the trial date is March 14, the last day to hear summary judgment motions is February 10 since February 11 is a court holiday and Department 220 does not hear motions on Fridays. 30 days notice from February 10 required defendant to file and serve its motion on or before January 11. Defendant filed and served its motion on January 12. Defendant's Notice of Errata cannot cure this untimeliness. The court lacks the authority to grant defendant's request to excuse its untimeliness. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
